question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only transaction template created by transactionOperationsReadOnly() by triggering a code path that bypasses the setReadOnly(true) flag, causing write operations to execute within what should be a read-only transaction, thereby allowing silent mutation of historical topic message records including runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open the maximum number of concurrent gRPC connections allowed by maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection and flood the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool, causing thread starvation that forces the server to serve stale or out-of-order historical topic messages with incorrect runningHash values to other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor configured in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause a race condition between concurrent subscribeTopic() calls, resulting in cross-subscriber contamination of TopicContext state and delivery of another subscriber's runningHash values as if they were the caller's own Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the TransactionTemplate created by transactionOperationsReadOnly() propagates an existing read-write transaction from an outer context, effectively upgrading the transaction isolation and allowing dirty reads of in-progress importer writes that contain partially-committed, tampered runningHash data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed ConsensusTopicQuery with a crafted topicID that causes the applicationTaskExecutor to queue an unbounded number of tasks, exhausting the executor and causing legitimate subscribers to receive incomplete Hashgraph history with missing sequence numbers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default PROPAGATION_REQUIRED behavior of the TransactionTemplate in transactionOperationsReadOnly() to join an existing transaction that has already read tampered topic message data, causing the read-only view to reflect manipulated runningHash values from a poisoned transaction context?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS enforcement in the NettyServerBuilder configuration within grpcServerConfigurer() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC stream, injecting fabricated ConsensusTopicResponse messages with forged runningHash and sequenceNumber values that appear to be legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure any per-connection authentication interceptor on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to subscribe to any topic and receive the full runningHash chain, then use that information to detect and prove tampering in the Hashgraph history without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user set maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection to its configured limit by opening parallel streams on a single connection, then send slow-reading requests that hold executor threads, causing a backpressure cascade that forces the server to drop or reorder topic messages in the live stream, corrupting the apparent Hashgraph history delivered to other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated rapid subscribeTopic() calls that each open a new read-only transaction via transactionOperationsReadOnly(), causing connection pool exhaustion in the underlying datasource, which forces subsequent transactions to read from a degraded or replica database that contains stale or inconsistent runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of message size limits in the NettyServerBuilder configuration within grpcServerConfigurer() to send an oversized ConsensusTopicQuery protobuf message that causes heap pressure, triggering GC pauses that result in out-of-order delivery of topic messages with mismatched sequenceNumber and runningHash pairs?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not set an explicit transaction isolation level, relying on the database default, to perform a non-repeatable read attack where two reads within the same subscription return different runningHash values for the same consensus timestamp, making Hashgraph history appear inconsistent?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor in grpcServerConfigurer() by submitting a large number of long-running subscribeTopic() requests that monopolize all executor threads, causing a denial of the Hashgraph history streaming service and preventing legitimate clients from verifying the integrity of the runningHash chain?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the read-only TransactionTemplate from transactionOperationsReadOnly() to be used in a context where the underlying JPA EntityManager has a first-level cache containing stale topic message entities, causing the subscriber to receive cached runningHash values that no longer match the current database state after a legitimate correction?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of flow control configuration in grpcServerConfigurer()'s NettyServerBuilder to overwhelm the server's inbound message queue, causing the server to process ConsensusTopicQuery requests out of order and deliver historical topic messages with incorrect sequenceNumber ordering that misrepresents the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure keepalive settings on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to hold idle connections open indefinitely, exhausting the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots and preventing legitimate clients from subscribing to verify Hashgraph history integrity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the PlatformTransactionManager injected into transactionOperationsReadOnly() is a JTA transaction manager that ignores the setReadOnly(true) hint, allowing the transaction to participate in a distributed transaction that includes write operations modifying topic message runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP rate limiting in grpcServerConfigurer() to open thousands of connections from a single IP, each consuming maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots, causing the applicationTaskExecutor to be saturated and forcing the server to serve corrupted or incomplete Hashgraph history to all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Qualifier('readOnly') TransactionOperations bean is shared across all concurrent requests to cause a transaction context leak, where one subscriber's read-only transaction context bleeds into another subscriber's context, causing the second subscriber to receive runningHash values from a different topic's Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a maximum connection age on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to maintain a persistent connection that continuously streams topic messages, and by timing the stream carefully, observe the exact moment when runningHash values change in a way that reveals tampering in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of explicit transaction timeout configuration in the TransactionTemplate created by transactionOperationsReadOnly() to hold a long-running read transaction open, causing database lock escalation that blocks the importer from writing new topic messages and creating artificial gaps in the Hashgraph history sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() configures the same applicationTaskExecutor for all gRPC service handlers, allowing a malicious subscriber to monopolize the executor with CPU-intensive requests and starve the ConsensusController's subscribeTopic() handler, causing delayed or reordered delivery of topic messages that misrepresents the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring proxy-less configuration (@Configuration(proxyBeanMethods = false)) in GrpcConfiguration to bypass bean lifecycle management, causing the transactionOperationsReadOnly() bean to be instantiated multiple times with different PlatformTransactionManager instances, leading to inconsistent transaction isolation and non-deterministic runningHash values being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of inbound message rate limiting in grpcServerConfigurer() to send a flood of ConsensusTopicQuery requests with different startTime values, causing the database connection pool to be saturated with concurrent read-only transactions that return inconsistent snapshots of the runningHash chain across different requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that transactionOperationsReadOnly() uses PROPAGATION_REQUIRED (default) rather than PROPAGATION_REQUIRES_NEW, causing the read-only transaction to join an outer read-write transaction if one exists in the call stack, and thereby observe uncommitted writes to topic message runningHash values that have not yet been validated by the importer?
